blocks.content: Alpheas's head was under the photon magic cast by Sad, whose forehead was already drenched in cold sweat.
Viltor Achain—an annoying man, but the power of his magic was beyond imagination.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: Phew. Such powerful mind control. This would still work even in this era.
At first, he had thought it would be easy. No matter how grand the title of Grand Sorcerer, forty years had passed. Magic had evolved over generations, and dark magic had become obsolete.
But Abyss Nova was so complex, even Sad, a beneficiary of cutting-edge magic, couldn't analyze it.
After twenty minutes, he finally felt the light energy penetrate the core of the darkness.
From then on, Sad pushed the light in with his utmost mental strength.
The dark veil lifted, and Alpheas's memories began to unfold brilliantly along the flow of light.
Forty years ago. Capital, Bashuka.
The Kingdom of Tormia was swept by an unprecedented magic craze.
The 12th king, Adolf XII, unlike his predecessors who revered military might, was overflowing with intellect and kindness.
Upon ascending the throne, he separated the magic department, which had been limited to military affairs, and gathered countless talents.
Talents from the provinces and even other countries came to Bashuka to study, filling the capital's streets daily with the voices of intellect.
Fresh graduates of the magic school gathering in taverns to debate by day was a common sight.
Gypsy-style clothing with bold colors was in fashion.
For women, hair as short as to expose the neckline was a symbol of intellect.
Meanwhile, men's hair grew long, reaching their waists.
Taverns saw factions form over academic clashes, with fistfights breaking out often enough that security had to drag people away.
The most famous tavern in Bashuka was 'The Homeland of Ancient Gods,' a rustic drinking hole.
Its grand hall held over 200 tables.
At the central podium, people frequently climbed up to expound their magical views.
All sorts of sounds converged, and the topics of chatter varied.
Though magical debates dominated, gossip circulating among nobles was also brewed in real-time at The Homeland of Ancient Gods.
It was an era of romance.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Oh my, kids! He's here, he's here! Lord Alpheas has arrived!

blocks.speaker: Woman at the tavern entrance
blocks.content: Oh my, kids! He's here, he's here! Lord Alpheas has arrived!
A woman at the tavern entrance made a fuss, and the girls at the tables shrieked as they turned to look.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hello, ladies! It's as lively as ever here!
The fair-haired, handsome youth, Alpheas, entered The Homeland of Ancient Gods.
Beside him was a young man with a buzzcut, out of fashion—Ozent Clump, Rian's grandfather.
Though Clump, a swordsman, wouldn't have cast invisibility magic, the girls treated him like air and clung to Alpheas.

blocks.speaker: Woman at the tavern
blocks.content: We've been waiting, Lord Alpheas! What magic will you teach us today?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: First, let's wet our throats. My tongue stiffens quickly if it's not soaked in alcohol.
Hahaha! So funny. Lord Alpheas is really amusing.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: If his current disciples heard this, they'd find it lame and mock him, but back then, Alpheas was a popular figure who could say anything and be understood.
The heir of a first-class family, top of his class at the magic school, handsome, and refined—was there any woman in the world who wouldn't like him?
Meanwhile, the men looked at Alpheas with distaste.
Even those not narrow-minded enough to envy others couldn't help but glare when discussing him, showing how much resentment he inspired among males.
The Light of the Mirhi Family.
That was Alpheas's official title at the time, but among those who knew him, he had another nickname.
Arrogant Alpheas.
Forty years ago.
Anyone in the magic world could tell Alpheas was a man chosen by the gods.
The problem was that he displayed his talent without regard for time or place.
Once an argument started, he'd see it through to the end, and if the opponent made even a minor mistake, he'd dig into it relentlessly until they conceded.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Only Clump knew Alpheas meant no harm.
Not arrogance, but purity. His passion was just too much for people.
In truth, Alpheas was the type to admit his mistakes without hesitation and celebrate the other's victory.
The only problem was that such occasions were rare.
As Alpheas waited for his beer, bored, he mingled with people at the tables.
Clump watched worriedly, but seeing no point in stopping him, shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Sarof, a native of the capital and one of Alpheas's few rivals, was greeted with raised hands.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Oh, right. Sarof, I heard you submitted a thesis to the royal palace? I skimmed it—quite interesting.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: What? Why are you reading my thesis?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Isn't it obvious? My field is photons too, and the royal librarian is my ardent fan. Anyway, good job.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: R-Really? How was it?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: You nailed the characteristics of light. Flawless, really. The part about energy concentration hit me hard.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: What nonsense? Light's wave nature is already experimentally verified.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: True, but I disagree. Recent results show too many gaps wave theory can't explain. Light might be closer to particles.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Hah! Resorting to gossip now, Alpheas? Countless critics have pointed out errors in particle experiments! If light were particles, how would you explain waves? They're entirely different concepts!

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: You're the one trusting critics! I've seen papers debunking particle experiment errors too. But their approach wasn't scientific. Isn't that the real error?

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Enough! Who asked you to critique my thesis? If you're so confident, write your own! Let the world laugh at your nonsense!

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I'm just worried. Even if it's rejected now, you can revise it later. What matters isn't the thesis but realizing the truth, right?

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Get lost! I won't even speak to you again!
Alpheas returned to his table, licking his lips.
Debating a rival in the same field was mutually beneficial, so he wasn't upset.
But Clump was worried.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: They'd first met at an inn in Bashuka.
Clump, also from Creas, had bonded with fellow provincials, and a year had passed since they started hanging out.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Frankly, it was unlucky.
Even as a swordsman, he could tell—how much worse must it be for rival mages?

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Alpheas, are you a fighting cock? If others can't do something, just let it be. Why insist on fixing it?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Where's the 'others' or 'me'? Wrong must be righted. That's intellect.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Honestly. Do you know what people call you? Arrogant Alpheas. That's your nickname now.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hahaha! I like that. Much better than 'The Light of Mirhi'—so cringe.
Magic was the domain of talent, and Alpheas had been blessed with overflowing talent.
He believed talent would shine no matter how hidden, so humility was just hypocrisy.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: This is the fate of geniuses. Come on, don't overthink it. Let's drink. Hey, Rafne, bring snacks fast. This guy eats more than a bear.

blocks.speaker: Rafne
blocks.content: Hohoho! If Lord Alpheas wishes, of course!
Rafne, the tavern maid, looked at him with adoration.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Clearly, she was smitten too.
Alpheas treated every woman like a lover, giving Clump a headache.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Not that he was in any deep relationships. His superficial charm had countless men missing their chances. Even beyond magic, Alpheas was public enemy number one among males.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Even Rafne? At least date properly. What's wrong with you?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Haha, as you know, I'm everyone's lover. And I'm not even trying. They just melt at my killer smile.
Alpheas stroked his chin and smirked.
Indeed, it was the kind of looks women went crazy for.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Would smiling like that really work?
Clump, who had no luck with women, experimentally curled his lips.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: How's this? Do I have a killer smile too?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Oh, yeah! A real killer smile—a murderer's. Pfft!

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: You wanna die?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hahaha! Just kidding. It was actually decent. Use that smile at the party later. Girls will swoon.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Party? Do I have to go? I'm not a mage.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Who said only mages can go? All walks of life will be there—network a bit. Connections matter as much as skill.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Funny coming from you.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: What can I do? You gotta climb high to make a difference. Just come. Who knows, you might find your other half there.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Suddenly, as if catching a cold, Clump coughed repeatedly.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Ahem! Fine, I'll tag along.
